title: Prototype subgraph abstraction slider
description: Treat graph subgraphs as adjustable abstraction components at render time: hiding contained nodes, rewiring external edges from the subgraph component, deduplicating repeated external edges, and applying the same projected model to Add info overlays and 3D.
decision_notes: Kept `subgraph` authoring-only by default; abstraction is opt-in via `render(model, { abstractionLevel })`, `RenderResult.setAbstractionLevel(level)`, `<archmap-viewer abstraction-level>`, and the playground/viewer slider.
Saved nested subgraph `parent` relationships in the parsed model so abstraction level can map to subgraph depth: level 1 collapses top-level subgraphs, level 2 collapses child subgraphs, and so on.

Implemented abstraction as a pre-layout model projection. This keeps 2D, 3D, and Add info overlays aligned because they all render from the same projected nodes, edges, diagnostics, data, identity, permission, zone, and boundary references.
Collapsed duplicate external edges by projected source/target pair. Internal edges inside a collapsed subgraph disappear with the hidden contained nodes.
verification: Browser check on http://127.0.0.1:4174/: small subgraph sample showed Abstraction slider max=1; level 1 rendered nodes Service/D/E and edges Service->D, Service->E.
notes: The initial playground seed has no subgraph, so the Abstraction control hides itself until the parsed source contains at least one subgraph.
This is intentionally a prototype trial. A future polish pass may add richer visual styling for abstraction nodes or finer edge-merge summaries.
